title: Research Claim — v1
description: One sourced claim in the research knowledge base. A claim carries a verdict, links to the source-note(s) that back it, and may anchor to an AU gov control. Claims are EVIDENCE, never decisions: the adopt/build-on/greenfield verdicts and ADRs are made by @architect/@pm plus owner ratification (see charter.md, evidence-vs-decision separation). Implements the citation/falsifiability contract in researcher.skill.md and trust-policy.md.

id.description: Stable claim identifier. Decisions (ADRs) cite these ids; the validator requires that every decision references claim ids.

statement.description: The claim itself, stated as a falsifiable proposition — not a recommendation. The researcher surfaces evidence; it never recommends.

verdict.description: verified = corroborated by >=2 independent sources of sufficient tier; plausible = supported but not fully corroborated; overhyped = a claim others assert that the evidence does NOT support (anti-hype guardrail); unverified = no adequate source found. An unsourced claim is 'unverified' and is never smuggled in as fact.

evidence.description: Source-note links backing this claim. Each item references a source-note id that must exist under sources/ and validate. May be empty ONLY when verdict = unverified.

source_id.description: id of a source-note under sources/. The validator resolves this reference.

quote.description: Verbatim supporting text from the source (attributed, not paraphrased).

retrieval_date.description: Date the supporting evidence was retrieved.

independent.description: True if this source is independent of the other evidence sources (not the same vendor/author). Used to satisfy the >=2-independent-sources rule for contested/verified claims.

contested.description: True if the claim is disputed across sources. Contested claims require >=2 INDEPENDENT evidence items (enforced by the validator).

falsifier.description: What evidence would overturn this verdict. Required discipline for verified/plausible claims — a claim with no stated falsifier is marketing, not research.

control_anchor.description: Optional link to an AU gov control in the control register (controls/). Makes the claim defensible by tracing to authoritative guidance.

framework.description: Authoritative framework (e.g. 'ACSC ISM', 'Essential Eight', 'PSPF', 'DTA Policy for Responsible Use of AI', 'NIST SSDF', 'NIST AI RMF', 'SLSA', 'SFIA'). Candidate map — not ratified until the owner promotes the control register.

relationship.description: How the claim relates to the control.

classification.description: PSPF classification of the claim. Defaults to OFFICIAL; inherit the most restrictive classification of its evidence.

trust.description: Origin trust label (untrusted-wins). A claim inherits the most restrictive trust label of its evidence; an untrusted claim cannot be canonical.

consumer_profile.description: Which consumer profile(s) the claim is scoped to. A finding that is right for one can fail another, so scope is explicit.

date.description: Date the claim was recorded/last assessed.

notes.description: Free-text analyst notes (counter-evidence acknowledged, scope caveats).
